# Autonomous mission loop

`CON-autonomous-mission-loop`

| kind | status |
|---|---|
| conops | draft |

A fixed-wing UAV is given a mission objective as natural-language text. An edge Vision Language Model (VLM) running on the on-board mission computer acts as the autonomy layer: at each decision point it receives a top-down map image showing aircraft position and issues a pixel-coordinate navigation command. The autopilot retains full control-loop authority; the VLM only issues high-level waypoint commands via MAVLink.
The mission computer must be explainable (every decision logged with reasoning), visually grounded (VLM reasons from the image, not from pre-encoded coordinates), and safe (altitude floors, RTL fallback on any failure, bounded state machine).
